text.title: Alignment Methods: SFT, DPO, ORPO, and Model Merging
text.summary: SFT vs. DPO vs. ORPO alignment method selection, dataset format requirements, TRL trainer code snippets, and mergekit model merging strategies.

rule.statement: Start with SFT; use DPO or ORPO only when you have chosen/rejected preference pairs and need to align for human preference beyond instruction following.
rule.rationale: SFT requires only demonstration data (easier to collect). DPO/ORPO require paired preference data — collecting rejection examples from production is non-trivial.

list.item: SFT (Supervised Fine-tuning): instruction + response pairs, simplest, most common baseline
DPO (Direct Preference Optimization): chosen/rejected pairs, no reward model needed, beta=0.1 KL penalty
ORPO (Odds Ratio Preference Optimization): 2024 method, even simpler than DPO, no reference model needed
RLHF: reward model + PPO training — complex, use only for safety-critical alignment

rule.statement: For DPO training, provide the exact same base model as the reference model — using a different model invalidates the KL divergence calculation.
rule.rationale: DPO measures distance from the reference model's distribution; a mismatched reference produces nonsensical KL penalties and unstable training.

example.code:
from trl import DPOTrainer, DPOConfig
# Dataset format: {"prompt": "...", "chosen": "preferred", "rejected": "worse"} dpo_config = DPOConfig( beta=0.1, # KL penalty strength learning_rate=5e-7, per_device_train_batch_size=4, gradient_accumulation_steps=4, num_train_epochs=1, ) trainer = DPOTrainer( model=model, ref_model=ref_model, args=dpo_config, train_dataset=dataset, tokenizer=tokenizer, ) trainer.train()
example.note: ref_model must be the same base model as model before any LoRA is applied.

rule.statement: Merge LoRA adapter into base model weights before deploying to vLLM or TGI using model.merge_and_unload() — PEFT adapters are not natively supported by all inference servers.
rule.rationale: vLLM and older TGI versions require a standard HF model, not a PEFT-wrapped model. Merge has zero quality loss and removes adapter overhead at inference.

example.code:
from peft import PeftModel from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM
base = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained( "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B", torch_dtype=torch.float16, device_map="auto" ) model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(base, "path/to/lora") merged = model.merge_and_unload() merged.save_pretrained("merged_model")
example.note: merge_and_unload() produces a standard HF model with no PEFT dependency.
